paper.title_en: Color-flavor locked quark stars in Randall–Sundrum II braneworld gravity
paper.abstract_en: We investigate the equilibrium structure and stability of color-flavor locked (CFL) quark stars in the Randall–Sundrum II (RS-II) braneworld scenario. The CFL equation of state, derived analytically at order O(ms2,Δ2) in the strange quark mass ms and the color-superconducting pairing gap Δ, is combined with the modified Tolman–Oppenheimer–Volkoff equations obtained under the Weyl fluid closure PE=wU at a fixed brane tension λ=1038dyncm−2. We survey Weyl parameters w∈{−3,−1,−0.1,0,0.2,2} and six physically admissible (B, Δ) combinations satisfying the Bodmer–Witten absolute stability criterion. Among the EOS combinations considered, only B=60MeVfm−3 and Δ=100MeV yield Mmax > 2 M⊙ at the adopted brane tension. A central result is the weak sensitivity of CFL stellar structure to the Weyl closure parameter w: Mmax varies by less than 0.06 M⊙ across the full range of w, in sharp contrast to the hadronic case studied by Castro, Alloy, and Menezes (Castro et al., 2014). This behavior is traced to the stiffness of the CFL equation of state, which renders the Weyl contribution to the effective pressure subdominant relative to the leading CFL term. The configurations satisfying the adopted static criteria obey the Buchdahl compactness bound and the causality condition cs2<1, while the adiabatic index remains above Γ=4/3 throughout the physically realized stellar interior.
